report_name: New Phishing Wave Uses Fake Browser Windows to Target Microsoft 365 Users

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The attack is initiated via a spearphishing link sent to Microsoft 365 users, which leads to the attacker-controlled webpage hosting the Browser-in-the-Browser element.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing for Information
attack_patterns.description: The ultimate goal of the Browser-in-the-Browser campaign is to trick the user into voluntarily entering their Microsoft 365 credentials into a fake login form.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious Link
attack_patterns.description: User execution of a malicious link is required to navigate to the phishing page where the fake browser window is rendered.

attack_patterns.name: Masquerading
attack_patterns.description: The Browser-in-the-Browser (BitB) technique uses HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to render a fake browser window that mimics a legitimate Microsoft authentication pop-up, including a fake address bar displaying a legitimate URL like 'login.microsoftonline.com'.

campaigns.name: Browser-in-the-Browser Phishing Campaign
campaigns.description: A wave of sophisticated phishing campaigns targeting Microsoft 365 users by employing the Browser-in-the-Browser (BitB) technique to steal credentials via a fake, but highly realistic, browser window rendered inside the actual browser tab.
campaigns.objective: Steal Microsoft 365 credentials for account takeover, BEC, and data exfiltration.

identities.name: Microsoft 365 Users
identities.description: Users of the Microsoft 365 suite are the primary targets of the Browser-in-the-Browser phishing campaigns.

infrastructure.name: Browser-in-the-Browser Phishing Page
infrastructure.description: An attacker-controlled webpage that uses HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to render a fake browser window designed to look like a legitimate Microsoft 365 login pop-up for credential harvesting.

indicators.name: Potential Browser-in-the-Browser Phishing Page
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Browser-in-the-Browser Phishing Page
id: 2b9a7c3d-8e4f-4b1a-9c8d-6f5e4d3c2b1a
status: experimental
description: Detects potential phishing pages that may be used for Browser-in-the-Browser (BitB) attacks by looking for URLs on non-Microsoft domains that contain Microsoft 365 login-related keywords.
references:
  - https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/06/14/week-in-review-exploited-check-point-vpn-zero-day-oracle-peoplesoft-servers-under-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-14
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.t1036
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - 'login'
      - 'microsoft'
      - 'office'
      - '365'
      - 'auth'
  filter:
    cs-host|endswith:
      - '.microsoft.com'
      - '.microsoftonline.com'
      - '.live.com'
      - '.office.com'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: This indicator detects web traffic to non-Microsoft domains that contain keywords related to Microsoft 365 login pages, a common characteristic of phishing sites setting up Browser-in-the-Browser attacks.
